findings.title: Dynamic import of user-reachable arch.ts executes arbitrary code in daemon process
findings.detail: InvariantRunner.runArch() calls import(pathToFileURL(targetPath).href) on worker-writable .ifleet/invariants/<repoSlug>/arch.ts. A jailbroken worker can write a malicious arch.ts that gets dynamically imported by the daemon process under full process credentials. The path-traversal check blocks .. and absolute paths but not file-content attacks.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace dynamic import with a sandboxed worker_threads runner or vm.runInContext with a restricted module loader. Allowlist which repos can declare arch.ts in config and validate file content before importing.

findings.title: TLS verification disabled for Supabase/PostgreSQL when CA cert path is not set
findings.detail: When SUPABASE_CA_CERT_PATH is unset and the URL contains 'supabase.co', the pool is created with ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling certificate validation on connections carrying knowledge-graph embeddings and audit findings. Trivially vulnerable to MITM interception.
findings.fix_sketch: Require SUPABASE_CA_CERT_PATH in production or use ssl: true (system CA bundle) instead of disabling verification. Throw or warn loudly and refuse pool creation when NODE_ENV=production and cert is absent.

findings.title: Public control plane cancel only marks store state; running pipeline worker is NOT killed
findings.detail: The onCancel handler on port 3001 writes blocked+cancelled to TaskStore but does NOT abort the in-flight pipeline worker (AbortController lives only in the daemon on port 3002). Operators receive HTTP 202 OK believing the task is cancelled while Claude continues consuming API quota and making commits. No timeout or watcher eventually kills the orphaned worker.
findings.fix_sketch: Route cancel commands exclusively to port 3002, or add a daemon watcher that notices blocked+cancelled in the store and triggers AbortController.abort() for the pipeline.

findings.title: Docker sandbox runs as --user root inside every container
findings.detail: buildDockerArgs() hard-codes --user root for all verification phases. A container-escape vulnerability gives an attacker root credentials on the host. The worktree is mounted at /work so file exfiltration is already possible post-escape.
findings.fix_sketch: Create a dedicated non-root user in the ifleet-verifier:base Docker image and use --user ifleet (UID 1000). Change /root/.pnpm-store to /home/ifleet/.pnpm-store in the image accordingly.

findings.title: Editor reviewer and doctor roles receive architect output as direct Claude instructions with no injection filter
findings.detail: In buildWorkerPool.spawn(), trustedBrief=true for all non-architect roles skips wrapBriefAsData(). If the architect is jailbroken or returns malicious output, that content becomes direct instructions for editor/reviewer/doctor with no sandboxing.
findings.fix_sketch: Apply a lightweight quoteAsUserData() wrapper to architect output before forwarding to editor/reviewer/doctor, or validate architect output conforms to expected JSON plan schema before passing it downstream.

findings.title: GitHub token exposed via git -c argument visible in proc cmdline and ps output
findings.detail: authPrefix() injects the GitHub token as -c http.https://github.com/.extraheader=AUTHORIZATION: bearer <TOKEN>. This argument is visible in /proc/<pid>/cmdline and to logging tools that capture command lines.
findings.fix_sketch: Use GIT_ASKPASS or a credential helper script that reads the token from a file. This keeps the token out of argv.

findings.title: Bash cat glob in worker allow list can exfiltrate arbitrary host files
findings.detail: The WORKER_CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS allow list includes Bash(cat *) with no path restriction. A worker can cat /etc/passwd, cat ~/.claude/credentials.json, or traverse to parent directories to read host-level secrets.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace Bash(cat *) with Bash(cat ./*) or restrict to the worktree path. Better yet remove cat from the allow list and rely on the Read tool (already allowed) for file reading.

findings.title: markCancelled in UnifiedQueueAdapter missing conditional fromState guard
findings.detail: markCompleted and markFailed pass fromState='in_flight' to store.updateState() so concurrent state changes are detected. markCancelled calls store.updateState without a fromState guard, meaning a completed or failed task can be overwritten with blocked+cancelled, corrupting the audit trail.
findings.fix_sketch: Add fromState='in_flight' as the fourth argument to store.updateState() in markCancelled, and check the returned boolean to skip sourceFor(task).markFailed() when the state was not updated.

findings.title: markFindingClosed writes index.json and closed.json non-atomically
findings.detail: markFindingClosed() calls writeAuditIndex first then appendToClosedJson separately without holding the index.json lock across both operations. A process crash between writes leaves index.json showing the finding as closed but closed.json missing the record.
findings.fix_sketch: Combine the index.json write and the closed.json append inside a single withClosedJsonLock critical section.

findings.title: GitHub drain loop has premature break on duplicate ID before exclusion set insertion
findings.detail: In GitHubIssuesSource.drain(), after pickNext the code checks if (exclude.has(next.id)) break before adding the ID to the exclude set. This causes the drain loop to terminate instead of continuing when pickNext returns a previously-seen ID.
findings.fix_sketch: Move exclude.add(next.id) to before the if (exclude.has(next.id)) break check, or remove the redundant guard since excludeIds handles dedup at the source.

findings.title: getAllPrDecisions defaults to 50000 rows with no streaming
findings.detail: TaskStore.getAllPrDecisions() defaults to limit=50000 rows fetched synchronously via better-sqlite3 all(). Loads all rows into a single JS array before returning, potentially consuming hundreds of MB of heap.
findings.fix_sketch: Add pagination with a before cursor on created_at or use iterate() to stream rows. Cap default at 1000.

findings.title: MemoryNonceLedger setInterval timer not unref-ed causing Node.js process hang in tests
findings.detail: MemoryNonceLedger starts a setInterval in its constructor. Tests that create a control plane but fail to call stop() leak the interval timer, preventing Node.js from exiting after the test suite completes.
findings.fix_sketch: Add .unref() on the interval timer so it does not prevent Node.js from exiting when no other work is pending.

findings.title: Discord broadcast messages silently dropped with no dead-letter log when outbox not configured
findings.detail: In broadcastIFleet(), when pendingCount >= MAX_QUEUE_DEPTH and _outbox is null, the message is dropped with only a console.warn. The outbox is not wired on the public control-plane process. Burst events during bot restart or Discord downtime are silently lost.
findings.fix_sketch: Record dropped messages to a persistent dead-letter log. Alternatively wire the outbox to the public server as well as the daemon.

findings.title: repoSlug hardcoded to IFleet in VerifierController preventing multi-repo invariant checking
findings.detail: In daemon.ts the VerifierController is constructed with repoSlug: 'IFleet' hardcoded. Invariants for any other managed repo are never run regardless of which repo the task targets.
findings.fix_sketch: Thread the task's repo field through VerifierController so repoSlug is derived from the task being verified rather than fixed at boot.

findings.title: Package imports cross package boundary via deep relative path traversals
findings.detail: interaction-create.ts imports ../../../../../src/audit/audit-store.js and ../../../../../src/orchestrator/approval-gate.js, escaping packages/orchestrator-core/ into main src/. Breaks monorepo isolation and creates fragile path resolution.
findings.fix_sketch: Expose required functions as a proper package dependency replacing relative path escapes with package imports.

findings.title: node_modules symlink in worktree allows workers to install packages affecting all concurrent tasks
findings.detail: setupWorktree() creates a symlink from worktree/node_modules to repoRoot/node_modules. Workers with Bash(pnpm *) can run pnpm install which resolves through the symlink to shared host node_modules, affecting all concurrent tasks in other worktrees.
findings.fix_sketch: Deny Bash(pnpm install *) specifically in the worker deny list, or use a per-worktree copy of node_modules rather than a symlink.

findings.title: wrapBriefAsData does not scrub BRIEF_DATA_MARKER_OPEN from user input enabling nested block injection
findings.detail: wrapBriefAsData() scrubs BRIEF_DATA_MARKER_CLOSE from user-supplied content but not BRIEF_DATA_MARKER_OPEN. An adversarial user can include the open marker to create confusing nested block structure, potentially causing the model to treat instruction-layer text as data.
findings.fix_sketch: Also scrub BRIEF_DATA_MARKER_OPEN from user input alongside the existing BRIEF_DATA_MARKER_CLOSE scrub.

findings.title: spawnCapture passes full process.env minus GIT_ vars exposing secrets to git subprocess hooks
findings.detail: spawnCapture() in repos/manager.ts passes most of process.env (including GITHUB_TOKEN, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, IFLEET_HMAC_SECRET) to the git subprocess, visible to any pre/post-commit hooks or credential helpers git invokes.
findings.fix_sketch: Apply the same minimal env allowlist used in claudeChildEnv() and verifyChildEnv(): pass only HOME, PATH, USER, LOGNAME, LANG, and required GIT_ auth vars.

findings.title: getKgPool caches pool module-level with no connection error handler
findings.detail: There is no pool.on('error', ...) handler on the cached pg Pool. Idle client errors cause unhandled promise rejections that can crash the daemon process.
findings.fix_sketch: Add cachedPool.on('error', (err) => console.error('[kg-pool] idle client error:', err)) after creating the pool.

findings.title: DiscordOutbox table DDL duplicated between discord-outbox.ts and store.ts SCHEMA risking silent drift
findings.detail: The discord_outbox table DDL appears in both packages/orchestrator-core/src/observability/discord-outbox.ts and packages/orchestrator-core/src/queue/store.ts SCHEMA. Schema drift between the two DDL strings would silently fail to apply the newer definition to existing databases.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the discord_outbox DDL from TaskStore SCHEMA and let DiscordOutbox own it entirely.

findings.title: verify and force_pr commands silently no-op on public control plane returning false 202 success
findings.detail: The onVerify and onForcePr handlers on the public server log to stderr but return undefined, while the HTTP layer still responds HTTP 202. The Discord bot tells the operator the command was accepted when it had no effect.
findings.fix_sketch: Return HTTP 503 with a descriptive body from these handlers on the public server, or remove them from the public server entirely.

findings.title: Verifier docker image ifleet-verifier:base assumed to exist with no build or pull probe at startup
findings.detail: DockerSandboxRunner defaults to image: 'ifleet-verifier:base' and never validates the image exists before running verification phases. If the image is missing, every phase fails with a cryptic Docker error.
findings.fix_sketch: Add an imageExists probe in probeDocker() and surface a clear error when the image is absent. Add a build/pull step to the VPS deployment script.

findings.title: ControlPlane HTTP server destroys nonce store before draining in-flight connections
findings.detail: In createControlPlane(), stop() calls nonceStore.destroy() synchronously before server.close(). In-flight requests at shutdown may call nonceStore.registerOrReject() after the ledger is torn down: for MemoryNonceLedger nonce dedup is silently disabled; for SqliteNonceLedger calls to a closed DB throw.
findings.fix_sketch: Call nonceStore.destroy() only after server.close() resolves (inside the close callback), ensuring all in-flight requests complete before the nonce store is torn down.

findings.title: pendingCount derived from stale in-memory snapshot can trigger spurious rate-pause
findings.detail: Line 356 of sprint.ts used tasks (the pre-dispatch snapshot) instead of freshTasks (loaded after dispatch loop) to count pending tasks. After dispatch transitions tasks to assigned, the stale snapshot still shows them as pending, triggering checkRateLimit unnecessarily.
findings.fix_sketch: Use freshTasks.filter() instead of tasks.filter() on line 356. Fixed in this audit run.

findings.title: durationMs inflated by pause time when sprint is paused and resumed before completing
findings.detail: sprint.completed payload computed durationMs as now - running.startedAt. Since startedAt is the original start time (preserved through pause/resume cycles), the metric includes all pause time. Added elapsedMs tracking to accumulate only active time.
findings.fix_sketch: Track elapsedMs in running/paused states; compute durationMs as (elapsedMs ?? 0) + (now - startedAt) where startedAt resets to now() on each resume. Fixed in this audit run.

findings.title: console.warn used for routine informational log messages throughout server.ts and daemon.ts
findings.detail: Both src/server.ts and src/orchestrator/daemon.ts use console.warn for normal startup messages. PM2 routes stderr separately from stdout, so these appear as warnings in PM2 logs causing noise and masking real warnings.
findings.fix_sketch: Use console.log for informational messages and reserve console.warn for genuinely unexpected but non-fatal conditions.

findings.title: TaskSource interface has no markCancelled causing cancelled tasks to appear as failed in GitHub and Discord
findings.detail: UnifiedQueueAdapter.markCancelled() calls sourceFor(task).markFailed() because TaskSource has no markCancelled method. GitHub issues and Discord threads receive a failed label even when the task was deliberately cancelled.
findings.fix_sketch: Add markCancelled(task: QueuedTask, reason: string): Promise<void> to the TaskSource interface and implement it in both GitHubIssuesSource and DiscordSource.

findings.title: Silently swallowed errors in void fire-and-forget chains in repos/manager.ts
findings.detail: packages/orchestrator-core/src/repos/manager.ts uses void settled.then(...) with no .catch() on some call sites, silently swallowing errors from lock cleanup logic.
findings.fix_sketch: Add explicit .catch((err) => console.error('[repo-manager] lock cleanup error:', err)) after settled.then() call sites.

findings.title: apps/wahq-bot/src/index.ts is an empty stub with only an export statement
findings.detail: The file contains only export {} with no implementation or roadmap reference, creating an empty package entrypoint that could confuse future contributors.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a comment explaining this is a stub pending the Agent SDK migration (referencing AGENTS.md).
